seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in Instructor Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in Instructor agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Instructor agents, RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization issues manifest as structured output validation failures when retrieved context leads to incomplete or hallucinated Pydantic model fields. Poor retrieval quality causes the LLM to generate partial extractions missing required fields or hallucinate enum values not present in retrieved documents. Instructor's validation retries exhaust quickly when RAG context lacks sufficient detail for accurate structured extraction.

howItManifests.scenario: Retrieved documents missing key entities for extraction
howItManifests.impact: Structured output validation retries exhausted after max_attempts

howItManifests.scenario: RAG returns irrelevant documents with outdated enum values
howItManifests.symptom: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
howItManifests.impact: ValidationError: value is not a valid enumeration member

howItManifests.scenario: Context truncation cuts off nested document structure
howItManifests.symptom: Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
howItManifests.impact: Pydantic validation fails on missing nested required fields

howItManifests.scenario: Low retrieval precision returns mixed document types
howItManifests.symptom: Inconsistent field presence across validation attempts
howItManifests.impact: Retry loop oscillates between partial successes and total failures

detection.name: Monitor Instructor Validation Retry Counts
detection.description: Track `max_retries` exhaustion and partial extraction rates in Instructor calls

detection.name: Log Pydantic Validation Error Patterns
detection.description: Capture specific validation errors indicating RAG quality issues

detection.name: Context Relevance Score Check
detection.description: Validate retrieved context contains expected extraction patterns
detection.code:
import re

def validate_rag_context(context: str, expected_entities: list[str]) -> float:
    '''Score context relevance for structured extraction'''
    context_lower = context.lower()
    entity_hits = sum(1 for entity in expected_entities 
                     if re.search(re.escape(entity.lower()), context_lower))
    
    # Check for extraction patterns
    title_pattern = r'\b(?:title|subject|document):?\s*[^\n]+'
    category_pattern = r'\b(?:contract|invoice|receipt)\b'
    
    patterns_found = len(re.findall(title_pattern, context)) + \
                    len(re.findall(category_pattern, context))
    
    relevance_score = (entity_hits + patterns_found) / (len(expected_entities) + 2)
    return relevance_score

# Alert if score < 0.6
if validate_rag_context(context, ['invoice', 'payment']) < 0.6:
    raise ValueError('RAG retrieval quality too low for extraction')

fixes.name: Hybrid Retrieval with Keyword Boosting
fixes.description: Boost retrieval using Pydantic field names and enum values as query expansion

fixes.name: Fallback Context Injection
fixes.description: Inject schema-valid enum values and field examples into prompt when retrieval fails

fixes.name: Multi-Stage Nested Extraction
fixes.description: Extract nested objects in stages to handle inconsistent retrieval depth

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically detects RAG quality issues in Instructor agents by monitoring Pydantic validation patterns, retry exhaustion rates, and extraction completeness metrics. When Instructor's `max_retries` exhausts or specific validation errors like enum hallucinations occur, Vex captures the full error context including retrieved documents and query patterns. 

Vex's agent reliability platform provides automated remediation by suggesting retrieval query expansions, fallback context injection, and hybrid retrieval strategies tuned to your Pydantic schemas. The platform tracks RAG quality KPIs across all Instructor calls and surfaces optimization opportunities with one-click fixes. 

Integration takes <5 minutes by wrapping your Instructor client with `vex.verify()`, giving you instant visibility into retrieval-extraction failure modes and automated improvement recommendations.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import verify
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI

# Install: pip install instructor vex-sdk

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

@vex.verify(
    failure_modes=[
        'instructor_retries_exhausted',
        'pydantic_enum_validation',
        'partial_extraction_missing_fields',
        'nested_depth_inconsistency'
    ],
    auto_remediate=['query_expansion', 'fallback_injection']
)
@client.chat.completions.create
def reliable_rag_extraction(prompt: str, context: str) -> Document:
    """Vex monitors and auto-fixes RAG quality issues"""
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        response_model=Document,
        messages=[
            {'role': 'system', 'content': 'Extract from context only:'},
            {'role': 'user', 'content': f"Context: {context}\n\n{prompt}"}
        ],
        max_retries=3
    )

# Vex auto-generates:
# - Retrieval quality dashboards
# - Query expansion recommendations
# - Schema-aware fallback prompts
# - Failure mode alerts

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
